what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment that links attachment att-eefc326e to a board item in the dragnet-development channel.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter with no body content.
The target is a board:// URI pointing to item-3480c157.
It includes an added timestamp in ISO 8601 format.
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme.
The file is purely a metadata link record with no payload.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
References external items with a custom board:// URI scheme.
Stores channel memory attachments as individual markdown files named by attachment id.
concerns: No payload or content is present beyond the link metadata.
The board:// URI cannot be resolved or validated from within this file alone.
The record may be an orphan without accompanying board item or attachment content.
notes: A minimal channel-memory metadata stub that associates attachment att-eefc326e with board item item-3480c157 in dragnet-development.
questions: What content or artifact does this attachment actually represent?
What data is stored in board item item-3480c157?
Is the board:// URI resolvable elsewhere in the system?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose appears to be a link record, and the file does exactly that; no mismatch.
apparent_themes: Traceability between attachments and board items
intention_signals: Persist a cross-reference between a channel attachment and a specific board item so the relationship can be retrieved and audited later.
contribution: A concrete, timestamped attachment-to-board-item reference of type board_items, preserving one relationship edge in the dragnet-development channel memory.
summary_for_embedding: Markdown metadata file declaring attachment att-eefc326e of type board_items in channel dragnet-development and targeting board item item-3480c157 with an added ISO timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: Record the association between a channel attachment and a specific board item for later traceability.
